steps.canonical_text: Note the time the seizure starts. Most seizures stop on their own within a few minutes.
Move sharp or hard objects away from them. Do not move the person unless they are in danger.
Do not hold them down and do not put anything in their mouth, including your fingers.
must_keep_keywords: do not put anything in their mouth

steps.canonical_text: Place something soft, like a folded jacket, under their head to protect it.
Once the seizure ends, gently turn them onto their side and check that their airway is clear. Stay with them until they are fully awake.
Call emergency services now if the seizure lasts more than 5 minutes, another starts right after, they do not wake up, they are injured, in water, or pregnant, or if this is their first seizure.
